description: Students explore French adjectives used to describe personality traits, including how masculine and feminine forms of those adjectives are formed.
Students describe their own personality and weekly activities, building on personality-trait adjectives and their masculine and feminine forms.
Students learn vocabulary for family members and reflect on the personal and changing nature of who they consider part of their own family.
Students explore vocabulary for introducing themselves in French, including stating their name, age, where they live, and the languages they speak.
Students explore the different types of buildings people live in, along with rooms of the home and the activities done in each one.
Imagining they work as an interior decorator, students learn bedroom furniture vocabulary and then describe a newly decorated bedroom to someone else.
Students explore the Carnaval de Québec celebration, meeting its mascot Bonhomme and learning vocabulary for activities you can do at the carnival.
Students continue exploring activities one can do at the Carnaval de Québec and practise naming them through interactive games.
Students consider the common elements shared by celebrations across Canada's diverse cultures and watch a video about one cultural celebration.
Students learn about National Indigenous Peoples Day on June 21 and pow-wows as a way some communities celebrate First Nations, Inuit, and Métis cultures.
Students explore laughter and humour while learning vocabulary for a range of emotions and matching each emotion to its corresponding adjective.
Students explore texts about playing tricks and telling jokes, review emotion-related vocabulary, and practise expressing themselves through dramatizations.
Students learn the names of animals found in a zoo, organized by pavilion (Africa, the Americas, and Canada), and check their understanding with a matching game.
Students learn to identify main ideas in a brochure and to create captions describing photos, supported by zoo-related vocabulary for animals, places, and people.

french_title: Explorons la vieille ville de Québec
description: Students explore the francophone Québec City, discovering notable places to visit such as the Château Frontenac and popular leisure activities.

french_title: Une visite à la ville de Québec
description: Students review popular places and leisure activities in Québec City and practise matching images to each location or activity.
Students learn expressions of courtesy and politeness in French, including using the formal pronoun "vous" when addressing adults outside one's family.
Students practise recognizing the months of the year in French by listening to audio clips and identifying the month named in each one.
Students explore the winter season and learn vocabulary for winter clothing such as a tuque, mittens, boots, and a coat through a video and activities.
Students explore winter festival activities, focusing on Ottawa's Winterlude (Bal de neige) festival, and learn related vocabulary while sharing their opinions.
Students explore different modes of transportation and the things to consider when travelling, starting with transportation vocabulary and flashcards.
Students compare different modes of transportation for travelling between Toronto and Québec City, learning comparison vocabulary for speed and slowness.

french_title: Se déplacer dans la ville de Montréal
description: Students explore places to visit in Montréal and the various modes of transportation for getting there, beginning with related vocabulary.
Students learn how to give directions in French, reflecting on how to explain the way to places in their community and watching a video on giving directions.
